description_long: This object is a vintage-style computer monitor with a boxy, off-white exterior and a prominent CRT display. The front features a thick, matte bezel framing a square screen, which displays a pixelated grayscale image and a small digital clock in the upper right corner. Below the screen, there is a horizontal strip with a colored 'Oculus' logo, adding a touch of branding to the otherwise utilitarian design. The sides are plain with subtle scuff marks, while the rear panel is detailed with ventilation grilles, various ports, a power socket, and regulatory labels. The monitor sits on a slightly protruding base, which is integrated into the main body and provides stability. The overall appearance suggests a robust, early personal computer monitor suitable for desktop use in an urban or office environment.
description: A vintage off-white CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, square display, and rear ventilation and ports. Features a colored 'Oculus' logo and a sturdy integrated base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square CRT screen, thick bezel, colored 'Oculus' logo, and a pixelated display image.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, slightly scuffed surface with no visible features except subtle panel lines.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing ventilation grilles, multiple ports, a power socket, and regulatory labels.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain, slightly scuffed surface and panel lines.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and off-white with a matte finish, featuring sharp corners and a simple, utilitarian style. It frames the display area with no decorative elements except for the colored logo below.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by an integrated, slightly protruding flat base that is part of the main body, made of the same off-white plastic material. No visible adjustability features are present.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are not clearly visible on the front, sides, or rear; any controls are likely minimal or integrated subtly, possibly along the lower front strip.
attribute_port_visibility: Visible on the rear panel, including multiple ports and a power socket.
attribute_brand_logo: A colored 'Oculus' logo is present on the lower left of the front bezel, featuring a small, multicolored emblem and text.
